title: helianthemum origanifolium subsp. andalusicum
intro: Helianthemum origanifolium subsp. andalusicum, also known as the Andalusian rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a succulent perennial plant that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as wound healing, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, making it a valuable remedy for skin issues and infections. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used to make salves and ointments for topical application.
